channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Donald Trump is reportedly set to pick the crypto enthusiast Paul Atkins to lead the SEC
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has tapped Paul Atkins to chair the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), according to crypto outlet Unchained. Assuming Atkins accepts the offer, crypto enthusiasts would have plenty of reason to rejoice.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: The Touch Bar is back, sort of...and it looks terrible
item.description: ENIAC is building a touch bar, called the Flexbar, for Macs and almost any other platform you can imagine.
item.dc:creator: lance.ulanoff@futurenet.com (Lance Ulanoff), TechRadar

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: Other than Bluesky’s own account, Ocasio-Cortez’s follower landmark is a first for a user, the platform told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: What to make of Pat Gelsinger’s abrupt exit
item.description: The retirement of Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has left more questions than Intel answered in its statement regarding his departure. Intel in the past had a measured process of ascension for future CEOs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AT&T sees earnings growth over next 3 years; eyes more than $40B in anticipated shareholder returns
item.description: AT&T anticipates earnings growth over the next three years thanks to the momentum of 5G and fiber services.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US issues new restrictions on chip manufacturing exports to China
item.description: The Biden administration issued new restrictions Monday on exports of certain semiconductor chips and equipment to China, marking the Biden administration's latest crackdown to curb the country's competitive advancements in chipmaking.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger retires
item.description: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger retired Sunday, after more than 40 years in the industry. The company quickly named two interim co-CEOs to hold the fort while it searches for a long-term replacement.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 5 ways AI will change the software development life cycle
item.description: Considering the scaling history and trajectory of generative AI models (specifically large language models, or LLMs) specialized for coding, the software development life cycle (SDLC) is ripe for disruption.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US police departments put EV patrol cars to the test
item.description: Some local police departments are considering moving to electric vehicles to bolster their fleet of patrol vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: Uber branches out into AI data labeling
item.description: Uber no longer offers just rides and deliveries: It’s created a new division hiring out gig workers to help enterprises with some of their AI model development work.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sharing is not always caring: 7 dangerous things to post online
item.description: Sharing information online like your vacation plans and relationship status can make you a target for scammers; here's what to avoid posting to keep yourself safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 10+ tips and services for new Bluesky users
item.description: For the past two or three weeks, upstart social network Bluesky has been attracting large numbers of new users every day, a crowd that quite evidently includes many Apple device users — the app has at times topped Apple’s App Store charts in the run-up to Thanksgiving in the US.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
